name: Sweet-Sour Meatballs Oriental
recipeIngredient: 1 can (20 oz) pineapple chunks, packed in juice
1 cup Fresh bread crumbs, about 2 slices bread
3 medium Scallions, cut into 1-inch pieces, about 3/4 cups
1 tbsp Light brown sugar, firmly packed
4 oz Fresh Chinese pea pods OR 6 oz Frozen pea pods, thawed
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the pineapple well and reserve 3/4 cup of juice. Set aside.
2. In a large bowl, combine beef, egg, bread crumbs, salt, and ginger. Mix with your hands or a wooden spoon until well blended.
3. Shape the mixture into 1 1/4-inch balls.
4. Heat vegetable oil in a 12-inch skillet over medium-high heat. Add meatballs and cook about 12 minutes, turning frequently until well browned on all sides.
5. Using a slotted spoon, remove the meatballs to a plate. To the drippings in the skillet, add scallions and garlic. Cook still over medium-high heat for about 5 minutes, stirring frequently until tender-crisp.
6. In a small bowl, stir the reserved pineapple juice into cornstarch until blended and smooth. Add this mixture to the skillet along with vinegar, sugar, and ground red pepper.
7. Increase the heat to high and bring to a boil. Stir for 1 minute.
8. Return meatballs to the skillet along with pineapple chunks and pea pods. Cook 3 to 5 minutes longer until heated through and pea pods are crisp-tender. Makes 4 servings.
